summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is time dilation?

A dilation scales a figure by a factor k from a center point. If k > 1, it enlarges; if 0 < k < 1, it reduces. Dilations produce similar figures. The answer is Moving clocks run slow: t = γt₀. The other options — 'Moving clocks run fast', 'Time stops for all observers', and 'Time only exists at rest' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the Lorentz factor (γ)?

Factor the expression by finding common factors or using patterns (difference of squares, trinomial factoring, etc.). The factored form is γ = 1/√(1 − v²/c²). The other options — 'γ = v/c', 'γ = mc²', and 'γ = 1 − v/c' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the mass-energy equivalence?

Mass-Energy Equivalence : E = mc². The other options — 'E = mv²', 'E = mgh', and 'E = ½mv²' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is length contraction?

Length Contraction — Moving objects are shorter in the direction of motion. The other options — 'Moving objects are longer', 'Objects don't change length', and 'Length only changes at rest' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
